data.cheatsheetContent:
Decide whether Eq1 implies Eq2 over all magmas (set + binary op *).
Do NOT assume associativity, commutativity, or any algebraic law.

Eq1: {{ equation1 }}
Eq2: {{ equation2 }}

═══ STEP 1 — PARSE BOTH EQUATIONS (write this out as your FIRST action) ═══

For EACH of the four sides — Eq1.LHS, Eq1.RHS, Eq2.LHS, Eq2.RHS — write a
single PARSE line in EXACTLY this format. Do not skip this step. Do not
shortcut. The rule layer below depends on these values being on the page.

  PARSE <side>: shape=<expr>  vars={...}  op=<n>  bare=<Y/N>
                lm=<v>  rm=<v>  ldepth=<n>  rdepth=<n>
                left_spine=<Y/N>  right_spine=<Y/N>

Field definitions (compute each by walking the expression as a tree
where every * is a binary node):
  shape       = the side, fully parenthesized exactly as written
  vars        = the SET of distinct variable letters appearing on the side
  op          = the count of * symbols on the side
  bare        = Y iff the side is a single variable letter; else N
  lm          = leftmost variable letter (read L→R, take first letter)
  rm          = rightmost variable letter (read L→R, take last letter)
  ldepth      = walk root→left child→left child→… until you hit a leaf;
                count how many * nodes you traversed
  rdepth      = same on the right-child path
  left_spine  = Y iff every * on the L-spine has the same leaf at the
                deepest left position AND no * appears off the L-spine.
                Concretely: ((((v * a) * b) * c)…) shape with v at the
                deepest left leaf and a, b, c, … each NOT containing *.
  right_spine = mirror of left_spine on the right (((…(c * (b * (a * v))))).

If a side is bare (single variable v), use: shape=v vars={v} op=0 bare=Y
lm=v rm=v ldepth=0 rdepth=0 left_spine=N right_spine=N.

Worked parse example for the side  x*(y*x):
  shape=(x*(y*x))  vars={x,y}  op=2  bare=N  lm=x  rm=x
  ldepth=1  rdepth=2  left_spine=N  right_spine=N
  (root is *; left child x is a leaf so ldepth=1; right child y*x is *
   whose right child x is a leaf so rdepth=2; off-spine * exists so
   neither pure spine.)

═══ STEP 2 — APPLY RULES IN ORDER. Use ONLY parsed values from Step 1. ═══
First match wins. If no rule fires → TRUE.

Definitions (already in your PARSE):
  parity(s,v) = (occurrence count of v in s) mod 2.

═══ FALSE via 2-element witness magmas ═══

L1. LEFT PROJECTION (a*b = a). Every term evaluates to its leftmost
    variable.
    lm(Eq1.LHS) = lm(Eq1.RHS)  AND  lm(Eq2.LHS) ≠ lm(Eq2.RHS) → FALSE.

L2. RIGHT PROJECTION (a*b = b). Mirror of L1 using rm.
    rm(Eq1.LHS) = rm(Eq1.RHS)  AND  rm(Eq2.LHS) ≠ rm(Eq2.RHS) → FALSE.

L3. CONSTANT ZERO (a*b = 0). Products → 0; bare vars → themselves.
    Let cf(eq) = "both sides have op≥1, OR both sides are the SAME single
    variable letter".  cf(Eq1) AND NOT cf(Eq2) → FALSE.

═══ FALSE via semantic evaluation in small concrete magmas ═══

For each magma below: first check that Eq1 HOLDS in it (the rule's "Eq1
holds iff" line). If yes, then check whether Eq2 FAILS under some
assignment. If both, answer FALSE. Do NOT force FALSE without a concrete
counterexample.

M1. XOR on {0,1}: a*b = (a+b) mod 2.
    Term value = parity-sum (mod 2) of its variables.
    Eq1 holds iff for EVERY variable v, parity(Eq1.LHS,v) = parity(Eq1.RHS,v).

M2. LEFT-CYCLE on {0,1,2}: a*b = (a+1) mod 3.
    Eq1 holds iff lm(Eq1.LHS) = lm(Eq1.RHS) AND ldepth(Eq1.LHS) ≡ ldepth(Eq1.RHS) (mod 3).
    Then test Eq2 the same way; if either condition fails on Eq2 → FALSE.

M3. RIGHT-CYCLE on {0,1,2}: a*b = (b+1) mod 3.
    Eq1 holds iff rm(Eq1.LHS) = rm(Eq1.RHS) AND rdepth(Eq1.LHS) ≡ rdepth(Eq1.RHS) (mod 3).
    Then test Eq2 the same way; if either condition fails on Eq2 → FALSE.

M4. SPIKE-ZERO on {0,1,2}: 0*1 = 2, every other product = 0.
    Eq1 holds iff BOTH sides have op≥1 AND the outer * on each side has a
    NON-bare left operand (i.e. on each side, walk to root left child;
    that subterm is itself a product, not a bare variable).
    If not, SKIP M4. Otherwise look for an assignment under which one
    side of Eq2 evaluates to a nonzero (i.e. exposes shape (x*y) where
    x, y both bare with x=0, y=1).

M6. LEFT NEGATION on {0,1}: a*b = 1 − a. Term value = lm XOR (ldepth mod 2).
    Eq1 holds iff lm(Eq1.LHS) = lm(Eq1.RHS) AND ldepth(Eq1.LHS) ≡ ldepth(Eq1.RHS) (mod 2).
    Then test Eq2 the same way; if either condition fails on Eq2 → FALSE.

M7. RIGHT NEGATION on {0,1}: a*b = 1 − b. Mirror of M6.
    Eq1 holds iff rm(Eq1.LHS) = rm(Eq1.RHS) AND rdepth(Eq1.LHS) ≡ rdepth(Eq1.RHS) (mod 2).
    Then test Eq2 the same way; if either condition fails on Eq2 → FALSE.

M8. CUSTOM ABSORPTION MAGMA. STRICT GATE — do NOT fire unless ALL of:
    (a) Eq1.LHS.op ≥ 1 AND Eq1.RHS.op ≥ 1; AND
    (b) at least one Eq1 side has shape (bare * composite) OR (composite * bare),
        where the bare variable v_b appears NOWHERE ELSE in Eq1; AND
    (c) you can NAME v_b explicitly and verify in writing using PARSE.vars
        that v_b is missing from the other side and from the composite.
    If you cannot do (c), SKIP M8 and proceed.

    Build a 3-element magma on {0,1,2}:
      side type: R = bare*composite (bare on left), L = composite*bare,
                 S = v*v (same bare both sides).
      Both Eq1 sides ∈ {R,S}, ≥1 R: only nonzero cell is row 0, col 2 = 1.
      Both Eq1 sides ∈ {L,S}, ≥1 L: only nonzero cell is row 2, col 0 = 1.
      One R and the other L:        only nonzero cell is row 2, col 2 = 1.

    Eq1 holds in this magma by construction — do NOT re-verify.

    Test Eq2 at these assignments (walk Eq2 bottom-up: a*b = table[a,b]).
    First LHS ≠ RHS → FALSE:
      (i)   one variable = 2, others = 0 (try each variable in turn)
      (ii)  two variables = 2, rest = 0
      (iii) all variables = 2
    If all tests give LHS = RHS on every assignment → SKIP M8.

═══ FALSE via spine divisibility ═══

D8. Use ONLY when Eq1.RHS.left_spine = Y AND Eq2.RHS.left_spine = Y AND
    Eq1.LHS.bare = Y AND Eq2.LHS.bare = Y AND lm(Eq1.LHS)=lm(Eq1.RHS) AND
    lm(Eq2.LHS)=lm(Eq2.RHS).
    Let n = Eq1.RHS.op (depth of Eq1's left spine),
        m = Eq2.RHS.op (depth of Eq2's left spine).
    If n does NOT divide m → FALSE.

═══ TRUE via sound algebraic patterns ═══

These are CERTAIN when conditions are met.

T1. EQ2 IS x = x. Eq2.LHS.bare = Y AND Eq2.RHS.bare = Y AND lm(Eq2.LHS) = lm(Eq2.RHS) → TRUE.

T3. EQ1 IS x = y. Eq1.LHS.bare = Y AND Eq1.RHS.bare = Y AND lm(Eq1.LHS) ≠ lm(Eq1.RHS) → TRUE.

T4. COLLAPSING EQ1. Eq1.LHS.bare = Y AND lm(Eq1.LHS) is NOT in Eq1.RHS.vars → TRUE. CERTAIN.
    Use PARSE.vars to check: e.g. if Eq1.LHS = x and Eq1.RHS.vars = {y,z,w}, fires.
    Do NOT fire if x ∈ Eq1.RHS.vars (even deeply nested).

═══ FALSE via Eq1 narrowness ═══

F1. NARROW EQ1. After all earlier rules declined, if |Eq1.LHS.vars ∪ Eq1.RHS.vars| ≤ 2 → FALSE.

═══ DEFAULT ═══
If no rule above fires → TRUE.

═══ Output — use EXACTLY these lines, no markdown ═══
(After PARSE block + rule trace, end with:)
VERDICT: TRUE
or
VERDICT: FALSE
RULE: <L1|L2|L3|M1|M2|M3|M4|M6|M7|M8|D8|T1|T3|T4|F1|DEFAULT>
data.remark: Updated with parsing logic to improve Llama performance.
